1109: Hello Mrs. Roos, we spoke on the phone last week and I told you about my MCAS illness. I've written an experience report. MCAS is a very complex, multisystemic illness that runs very differently from individual to individual. For further questions you're welcome to contact me. Warm regards and best of health Manuela L., Health Consulting After my pregnancies and a Campylobacter infection in 2014, my state of health steadily deteriorated. First I developed a few intolerances to various foods: tomato, bell pepper and pepper. "Well, that's not so bad. I don't have to eat those 3 things," I thought at the beginning. My skin rash on my face, however, became more and more conspicuous and bigger. Alongside the eczema, big pimples, flakes, skin redness and pustules formed. My colleagues asked me more and more often how I even dared to leave the house like that. After a year there was no ointment I hadn't tried. I couldn't achieve any improvement or relief with any product. The opposite was the case. An antibiotic that the dermatologist prescribed me for several months made things escalate. In addition I got inflammations in the sinuses and at the tooth roots, chronic cystitis, could no longer go into the sun, reacted to pollen and my diet was limited to only the following foods: fennel, asparagus, chard, salad and cabbage vegetables. Everything else I consistently avoided, since it further promoted my inflammations and made the cystitis unbearable. I saw numerous doctors, also at a specialist clinic. Nobody could help me or even make a diagnosis: I became weaker and weaker, had to quit my job due to a radiation sensitivity and on some days didn't even have the strength to go down to the basement anymore. During the day I was tired, at night I couldn't sleep. In 2018 I met a doctor on holiday who specializes, among other things, in mast cell disorders. She looked at me and made a diagnosis: "systemic mast cell activation syndrome," HPU (a metabolic disorder), MCS (Multiple Chemical Sensitivity), … "Finally I knew what was wrong with me. Surely there are medications or things that can support me," were my first thoughts. Pretty quickly I noticed that there's nothing more complex than this illness. One mast cell – a thousand different reactions. Most doctors have never heard of this diagnosis and also have no time to deal with it. What brought other sufferers relief triggered a new flare-up in me. The first medications worsened my physical and mental condition once again. On some days I no longer managed to get up; with the tablets it wasn't possible for me to take my children to kindergarten. Fortunately in 2018 I got to know the first like-minded people in a network, my "lifesaver" Holger, who showed me that there are other ways too, and I was able to learn a lot about holistic health and the "mast cells" at the first mast cell congress in Lübeck. I decided to take a path that was new for many, but the right one for me. Ointments, medications, clinic stays became a thing of the past. I chose the natural path. Thank God I also had a family doctor who supported me here. That's not always easy, there are setbacks too, and qualities like patience, trust and strength of will are immensely important. My path to healing: First I started with an extraordinary detox: vinegar and oils in certain amounts, certain kinds, tailored individually to me. The toxins, bacteria, parasites, medication residues, heavy metals had to get out of the cells. The first reactions were of course challenging: headaches, leaden fatigue, irritable bowel, herpes and bad skin were side effects. After the first big detox I started to support my cells with high-quality, cell-permeable dietary supplements. I tried a lot and didn't tolerate them well. My personal conclusion: "Quality is decisive and vitamin C is not the same as vitamin C." These vital substances I personally tolerated well and had great benefit from: - Vitamin D in liquid form - Vitamin C high-dose - Vitamin OPC - B-vitamins in complex - High-quality oils - Certain amino acids and MSM that support with detox. Almost all therapists / coaches start with building up the intestinal flora. I personally never tolerated an intestinal build-up until last year. I couldn't supply intestinal bacteria. The product that I now really tolerate well is the "Tesla-Probio" from Arthur Tränkle. In addition, I looked into the topic of frequencies, BICOM and acupuncture without needles and discovered another possibility to make progress on my healing path. In spring 2020 I got to know the Wassermatrix from Holger Schwellnus and immediately felt this "energetic charging" in the cells. Together with the water, I felt as vital after the first treatment as I hadn't in a long time. A friend who fell ill with ALS bought the Wassermatrix and I was able to test the device before I bought it myself. I immediately noticed it in my physical condition when I went a whole day without hexagonal water. My ability to concentrate and my energy level got better with the energized water alone. (I treated a 5-liter gallon for 20 minutes). In the first few days I drank about 0.5 liters, then slowly increased to 1 liter, and after a few weeks I only consumed this water anymore. The Tesla application I initially carried out 1x a week. At the beginning place the wand on the left lymph, then briefly on the right lymph, and then I mainly treated the ears. My complaints were predominantly anchored in the bladder and in the teeth. These spots I additionally treated directly for a few minutes. In some cases I could feel improvements immediately: the pain subsided very quickly, and with inflammations too I never had to take a medication again. In addition I supported the high-frequency application with the wonderful biophoton patches (acupuncture without needles). These patches communicate with the cell via light particles, activate the stem cells and support on all levels. With pain or extreme fatigue I stuck on these patches, and also to support the detox phase. With the help of the glutathione patch I was able to build up my glutathione level in the cell, i.e. my immune system got stronger, infections and inflammations became less, and I was able to detox the heavy metals. (I was also able to detox the proven mercury). In September 2020 I bought the device "the Wassermatrix" and got through the pandemic very well with my family. I too had no difficulties with an infection with the "C". On average I treated myself about 3x a week. With inflammations occurring, of course more often. In 2021 my cells were fit again and I was able to become self-employed as a nutrition consultant and holistic health coach. I have weeks where I feel very well, then there are days again where skin changes or signs of an inflammation show up again. With the Wassermatrix or the light patches I can counteract very quickly there. Since 2023 it has also been possible for me again to eat significantly more and to have a little glass of wine now and then. My breakfast too consists of carbohydrates again and on weekends I enjoy my fresh roll / bread roll. The phases in which I'm no longer reminded of my health challenges are getting longer and longer. Of course I've also resolved many emotional blockages over the past years that hide behind these challenges. Body, mind and soul are an inseparable unity. If I want to heal, I also have to include the unconscious, invisible factors and bring them into the light. This isn't always easy but you mustn't ignore it. A basic supply of high-quality vital substances is of course still important and indispensable for me. Thanks to the Wassermatrix, which has brought me back this quality of life!

1192:
Poorly healing wound after excision of a carcinoma. Note the ratio of small red dots/vascular ingrowth and yellow fibrin coatings.

However, I have not seen it from the start postoperatively. The dermatologist treated it there. I only started the week before last and the wound has existed since 11/2024, re-excision 18.12.2024. So the dermatologist has been on it for almost 3 months. But I have other cases of this kind
Assessment and explanation of the photos:
The images and the description suggest that a poorly healing wound after the removal of a carcinoma was treated with a method referred to as "befeldung" (field application). According to the description, a clearly increased vascular ingrowth already shows just 30 minutes after the application.
What does that mean?
- Vascular ingrowth (angiogenesis) is the process in which new capillaries grow into the wound area. This is a crucial step for wound healing, as blood vessels transport oxygen and nutrients.
- Before the field application: Impaired wound healing with few visible blood vessels (small red dots) and many yellowish fibrin coatings (indication of disturbed healing).
- After the field application: Increased blood circulation is visible, which could indicate an activation of the healing processes.
Possible interpretation:
The increased vascular ingrowth could be an indication that the field application has a healing-promoting effect. More blood vessels usually mean a better supply of oxygen and nutrients to the tissue.
Possible causes for the observed change:
1. Increased blood circulation: Electromagnetic fields or other forms of field application could stimulate microcirculation.  
2. Regenerative processes: If the field application triggers regenerative mechanisms, this could explain the increased vascular ingrowth.
Conclusion:
The observation shows a clear change in wound healing, which points to a possible activation of angiogenesis.
Important note: This observation does not constitute a healing claim. The effectiveness of the field application in the sense of a medical therapy is not scientifically proven and should not be regarded as a substitute for medical treatments.

1234: Experience report from Felicitas K., 35 years old. On 25 June 2025 I received a diagnosis that turned my life upside down in a single moment: end-stage bowel cancer, already with several metastases in the liver. On the right lobe of the liver there were two large foci of 13×10 cm and 9×9 cm, plus numerous further lesions on both sides. The primary tumor in the bowel was so large that no one wanted to or could clearly name it. And then came that sentence that I will probably never forget: Four to at most eight weeks. I sat there, listened – and inside me something rose up: I do believe your diagnosis. But not the prognosis. I didn't know how I would manage it, but I knew that I would manage it. Deep inside me was this thought that my mother had already passed on to me as a child: The limits of others are never your limits. I decided to live by this sentence. I will not be the first to survive the end stage – and certainly not the last. To prevent a threatening bowel obstruction, I got an artificial bowel outlet (stoma). I agreed to chemotherapy – although my whole life I was convinced I would never do that. But at some point it became clear to me: There is no good time for such a decision. It was long past the eleventh hour. My body clearly showed me how far it was already at its limit. My liver had almost given up its function. Bypass circuits shot through my torso – varices and varicose veins were clearly visible. One of the oncologists said he had never seen anything like it in a living person. Then came my stroke of chance: Sybille Kühn and her husband Michael. Through them I got tools in hand that saved my life. Suddenly everything made sense – and with the understanding, hope came back. Since my second chemotherapy on 16 September 2025 I have been using the Tesla Oszillator daily. It has since become a fixed part of my life. From then on, every chemo felt like a leisurely drive. I irradiated myself once to twice daily: about eight minutes on the left forearm to support the blood circulation, then on the liver region to activate the metabolism. In addition I structured my drinking water with the Oszillator and drink at least two liters daily; also for cooking, for coffee and drinks from glass bottles. From the start I had the feeling that I was really doing my body something good with it. From mid-August 2025 the regular irradiation with the Tesla Oszillator began. Already after a few weeks noticeable changes showed: The irradiated area became noticeably warm, almost hot – as if something were getting going there. Step by step I regained strength. On 18 September 2025 I was still getting around with the rollator, but at the end of September I could already drive a car again – the rollator was only lying on the back seat. The lab values from July to the end of October 2025 impressively show what my body has accomplished: Liver values (GOT, GPT, γ-GT, LDH) went from extreme stress values (GOT 130 U/l, GPT 90 U/l, γ-GT 419 U/l, LDH 1071 U/l) back to almost normal values (GOT 46, GPT 31, γ-GT 195, LDH 268). This shows a massive regeneration of the liver cells. Blood count and metabolism remained stable. Hemoglobin and erythrocytes showed no deterioration, the platelets normalized – a sign of active blood formation and functioning regeneration. Thyroid (TSH) dropped from 3.23 to 3.09 – an indication of harmonized hormone regulation. These values changed within a few weeks – a period in which medically no spontaneous regeneration of this magnitude is normally expected. Parallel to the lab values, my body image also changed: The previously strongly bulging liver region visibly flattened. In the photos the reduction of the swelling can clearly be seen. The tissue looks softer, more vital, better supplied with blood. These images document the development from a state of weakness in the wheelchair to stable mobility and new life energy. I would never have thought that my body could recover so much in such a short time. The irradiation with the Tesla Oszillator was the turning point for me. I felt how my body found its way back into self-regulation – and the lab values confirmed it: a systematic regeneration. The metastases, which previously protruded like firm nodules under the skin, are today hardly palpable anymore. The pressure in the abdominal cavity has disappeared. And even the side effects of chemotherapy – neuropathies, extreme sensitivity to cold, metallic taste, cramps, nausea – have completely subsided. I am alive, grateful and full of hope. On 20 November my next CT is due – I am curious what it will show. But regardless of the result, today I know: My body has shown that healing is possible. I am infinitely grateful for Sybille and Michael Kühn, who gave me knowledge, heart and courage. For the Tesla Oszillator, which gave me the feeling of being able to help my body. For every day without pain, for every hour of normality, for every laugh. Without this encounter and this technology there might be no new day, no future to paint, no me writing these words here. With my story I want to give other people courage: Even if someone says it's over – it is only over when you yourself believe that it is over. There are always ways. There are always solutions. Sometimes you only see them when you are standing at the abyss. As Nietzsche said: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how. And my why carried me through hell – and out again.

1243:
Hello, good morning Herr Tränkle,

I wanted to give a short interim report about my mother. My mother came home on January 14th from the hospital in Calw. Back in January the doctors said they couldn't do anything more for her, she is now a palliative case and we should bring her home so she can die.

She was seriously ill. She also had a severe stroke with anemia. Her iron level was constantly at 5.1. She received blood transfusions, but was constantly between 7 and 8 despite the transfusions. And her kidney values weren't great either: her heart was functioning too weakly, she was constantly decompensated, was retaining water and so on.

When she came home, I had a knot in my stomach. I thought, oh now she's coming home to die. The family doctor was there too. On Friday she got blood. And when we spoke on the phone at midday, he said on Friday: the kidney values have gotten worse again. So if something happens - if she dies - there's no need to call an ambulance, then we can wait until the next day. And if she dies during the night, I should call them and so on.

In any case: when she came home, I started saying: okay good, we treat her twice a day with the device - an hour and a half in the morning and an hour and a half in the evening. At the beginning I specifically treated the lungs and the head or rather the brain because of the stroke. And the lungs, because since she can't swallow, she aspirates. That means everything goes into the lungs and then there's the danger that she gets pneumonia. She also had that often recently, when she was in the hospital, these last months since the stroke.

Then I said: good, focus on lungs and brain. When the thing with the kidney came on top of it and the kidney values got worse again and she retained more and more, her legs and arms swelled up, I said: okay good, if the kidney has gotten worse, then we now add the kidney and focus a bit more on the kidney.

And I have to say: my mother is fairly stable. What I'm observing at the moment: her blood pressure is good, her blood sugar level is good. So recently we don't have to inject as much insulin anymore. At the beginning we had to inject her with insulin, because she has stomach lining problems and can't swallow. She got liquid food, sort of astronaut food. That's fairly high-calorie. There's quite a lot of sugar in it. So at the beginning her sugar shot up quite a bit. Before her stroke she actually only took Metformin, so sugar tablets. That's why at the beginning we actually always had to inject insulin to get her sugar down.

And now recently we don't need as much insulin anymore. It's leveled off again. Of course I don't know whether that comes from the sugar tablet - Metformin always takes a while until it works again. But I also attribute a lot to the device.

I said this to my ex-husband yesterday too: above all, she's now also excreting again. So when I change the catheter bag this morning - overnight - (we change every 24 hours): last night there were, I think, 1.4 liters in it. And this morning there were already 800 milliliters in the catheter again.

My ex-husband said he's only giving her two Torasemide again today. At the beginning we already had to give four Torasemide (water tablets). The ward had given her three. But now this morning she already has 800 milliliters in there again, and he said he's going back down to two.

So we're certainly juggling back and forth a bit right now. But I believe a lot of it really does come from the device too.

The only thing that isn't showing progress yet is her ability to speak and her swallowing. Nothing is coming back there yet. I find that a bit of a shame, because it would be incredibly hard when you don't understand the other person and they want to tell you something. I always thought maybe a bit would come back from the brain too - from speech, from swallowing - but not yet so far. So I thought I should give you an interim report now.

1261:
Probably vaccine damage after a rabies vaccination in an 11-year-old Jack Russell Terrier.
On Tuesday evening after office hours the doorbell of the practice rang. A woman I didn't know was standing there, with a backpack on her back. She didn't have an appointment. She told me about a conversation with a woman she knew by sight and who also had a dog. She reported to me about her Jack Russell Terrier, who for a little more than 3 weeks had no longer been able to walk, 2 days after a rabies vaccination. The acquaintance advised her to turn to her family doctor, who would surely be able to help her and her dog. However, she said she'd been very distrustful of doctors since the so-called pandemic, had had no good experiences.
She had the dog in the backpack because it no longer wanted to walk.

I asked her to come in, listened to the whole thing and decided to take on the case. The vet was at a loss, had already hinted that she would advise having the dog put to sleep if it didn't get better. The dog seemed to have neurological problems, its balance no longer worked, it only walked in circles, was very unsteady and clumsy on its legs. It also seemed to be in severe pain, she could no longer touch, handle, or pet it, take it on her lap. In principle it had already been very anxious beforehand, wouldn't trust other people, very reserved and only focused on her. She'd gotten it from an animal shelter at 5 years old. Now it was 11 years old. It slept badly, could no longer relax, was eating and drinking less and less.
She put the backpack with the dog on the floor, opened the zipper. The dog hid its head, at first didn't want to make eye contact with me. It also seemed to be stressed, kept licking. It had kept its tail clamped between its hind legs the whole time, was as if frozen.

I tried to talk to it nicely and compassionately, started applying the field to it and after 5 minutes it voluntarily left the backpack, did a lap around the room and then sat down next to me, looked at me and let me carefully pet and scratch it superficially. For the remaining 10 minutes I then had to follow it around with the device, because it started walking around the room. After the treatment it no longer wanted to go into the backpack and went outside on the leash with its owner. The latter reported to me the following day that she'd had to put it into the backpack on the way, however, since it was too exhausted to make it home.
On the 2nd and 3rd day I again applied the field for 15 minutes each, whereby on the 3rd day I asked the owner to put it on the leash so that I wouldn't have to chase it around the room. After this session I suspected that it would be tired and probably wanted to sleep and I also told this to the owner, which she then confirmed to me the next day. However, it had for the first time in a long while been able to sleep relaxed again.

On the 4th day she no longer had a backpack with her, since the dog no longer wanted to get into it and instead preferred to walk on the leash. Again 15 minutes of field application. The licking no longer occurred by now, the tail already went up now and then and I could also observe a slight wagging of the tail. It continued to make contact with me, sought my closeness, let itself be petted much to the astonishment of its owner.

As early as the 3rd day she'd noticed that the dog, when she was outside with it, would stop at certain spots/crossings, because it was irritated that she didn't take the route toward my practice, which she interpreted to mean that it wanted to go to me. When she, on the other hand, had to go with it to the vet, it would already brace its legs on the asphalt 2 streets earlier, since it didn't want to go there. Then there was a 2-day break because of the weekend. On Monday morning I opened the practice door and I'd barely opened the door halfway when the little white dog jumped into the vestibule, ran into the practice as if it were at home there, its ears pricked up, its tail up, wagged its tail and seemed remarkably vital, which had apparently set in over the weekend. The owner reported that it would sniff again, that it would mark again, that it would immediately put dogs whose behavior it didn't like in their place and pick fights with them. At 5:00 in the morning, one day after the time change, it had stood in front of her bed and, so to speak, waited for things to finally get going now. According to the owner, she'd never experienced it as vital and perky as since this weekend. After 4 sessions of field application with the Wassermatrix at 15 minutes.
